arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers wary of Brugge threat but backs Celtic Park’s power
arts.sum: Hoops chasing fourth straight home win in Europe Champions League progress will define club’s season Caution from Brendan Rodgers over Celtic’s prospects in the Champions League is perfectly understandable. Thoroughbred racehorses know how to time their run. That Bayern Munich, Real Madrid, Atlético Madrid, Milan, Benfica and Paris Saint-Germain started this week behind Celtic in the newfangled version of European football’s premier club competition is likely to prove a temporary impasse. Celtic, despite a hugely promising start, were 15th before Tuesday evening’s fixtures. The Scottish champions have not altered their goal of reaching the playoff round. Seven points from a possible 12 has not changed aspirations that existed before a ball was kicked in anger. Rodgers revelled in Celtic’s dismantling of RB Leipzig in the last round. Pre-match media duties for the subsequent Scottish Premiership stop at Kilmarnock were dominated by what had transpired days earlier. This was fair; Celtic have taken such public kickings for failures in Europe over recent years that they were entitled to purr over happier times. The performance against the German club was exceptional and a justification of Rodgers’ decision to return for a second Celtic tenure in 2023. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: 'Madness'... Graeme Shinnie reacts to what 'people' are saying about Aberdeen's challenge to Celtic
arts.sum: “I wouldn’t say we’re trying to put pressure on Celtic,” continued Shinnie, “It’s about winning as many games as we can and picking up points. “It’s up to us to carry that pressure, that’s what it’s like being at this club.

arts.ttl: The 2 hidden Belgian football insiders in the Celtic camp as Rodgers demands Leipzig levels to blow Brugge away
arts.sum: Arne Engels and Kasper Schmeichel both know the Belgian game well

arts.ttl: Cameron Carter-Vickers admits Celtic 'pretty close' to Champions League wish as loftier goals cited
arts.sum: Speaking ahead of the tie, Carter-Vickers has admitted Celtic are ‘pretty close’ to achieving their initial goal in the Champions League, which was to claim roughly ten points from their league phase fixtures. Cameron Carter-Vickers has explored Celtic’s goals for the remainder of the Champions League league phase as the Bhoys prepare to take on Club Brugge tomorrow night.

arts.ttl: James McFadden amazed by 'sensational' Celtic star's improvement alongside Nicolas Kuhn
arts.sum: But now that the Celtic players, who thrived under Ange Postecoglou, have had 12 months to adapt to new methods, as well as Rodgers working his own magic, then it is resulting in some fantastic performances and results. It’s a player that wants to improve.

arts.ttl: Hope and heartache at core of Club Brugge story

arts.ttl: Cameron Carter-Vickers believes Celtic have grown stronger in the Champions League
arts.sum: Cameron Carter Vickers knows Celtic are capable of holding their own in Europe.

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers channels the 'power' of Celtic Park as he goes after rare 4 in a row in the Champions League
arts.sum: The Hoops boss is gearing up for another massive night of European action in the east end of Glasgow

arts.ttl: The magic number of points Celtic believe they need to achieve Champions League qualification
arts.sum: Defender Cameron Carter-Vickers has told how the players set themselves a target at the outset

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers cites Leipzig reference point as Celtic look to give Brugge a 'really difficult night'
arts.sum: Brendan Rodgers has laid the gauntlet down to his Celtic side as they prepare to take on Club Brugge in the Champions League tomorrow night. “But I know this club and I know the fans and I know the stadium, so I know how special it really is and how difficult is can be, but what you have to do is match that with the performance and that’s something that has really pleased me in these recent home games in the Champions League.
